---
title: "LRP90: Mechanism model — phonological memory (RW) -> word reading (W)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP90 estimates the adjusted association between phonological memory (word and
nonword repetition, the ERB total) and word reading using *all three phase
transitions* (t1$\to$t2, t2$\to$t3, t3$\to$t4) with phase-specific intercepts —
completing the #311 trio after LRP88 (TR$\to$W) and LRP89 (TE$\to$W)
(descriptive-association workstream #314).

::: {.callout-note title="Covariate exposure (estimand note)"}
The exposure is the raw ERB total (`erbto`), an integer score whose documented
test maximum is recorded nowhere in the project — so it cannot honestly enter the
Beta-Binomial logit machinery as a bounded-count measure (a denominator would have
to be invented). It enters instead as a **standardised continuous covariate**:
$\beta_{\text{mech}}$ is the adjusted association per **+1 SD of the observed ERB
total** (the raw-score size of that SD is recorded in `config.json` as
`mechanism_exposure_sd_raw`). Rows where the ERB total was not measured are
**dropped**, not imputed — imputation with a missingness flag is an adjuster
policy, never acceptable for the exposure itself. The mechanism is necessarily
**linear** — a single slope, not a shape.
:::

## Adjustment set

Parents of $RW$ under the revised DAG (2026-07-10): $\{A, GA, HS\}$ — and nothing
else.

- $G$ — group; always conditioned on via $\beta_G$.
- $A$ — age; conditioned on linearly (a $\gamma_A\,z(\text{age})$ term).
- $HS$ — hearing status (`hs` / `hs_missing`), $RW$'s only measured parent;
  blocks every $RW \leftarrow HS \to \{TR, RV, TE, EV, SP, PA, LS\} \to W$ route
  at its root.
- $W_{\text{pre}}$ — baseline word reading (temporal precedence; not a collider).

::: {.callout-note title="No session-dose backdoor (contrast with LRP88/LRP89)"}
The DAG has no $IG \to RW$ or $IS \to RW$ edge: under the locked structure the
intervention does not move phonological memory, so session dose is **not** a
common cause of exposure and outcome here, and the flagged-IS caveat that LRP88
and LRP89 carry does not apply. $RW$'s variation is not intervention-generated —
in that respect this is the cleanest adjustment set of the #311 trio.
:::

$TR$, $TE$, $RV$, $EV$, $PA$, $NW$ and $PS$ are *descendants* of $RW$
($RW \to \{TE, EV, TR, RV, PA, NW, PS\}$) that also affect $W$; conditioning on
them would block legitimate indirect paths (e.g. $RW \to PA \to NW \to W$) and
pull the slope toward the direct-only component, so they are **not** in the
adjustment set. $SP$ is neither a parent nor a descendant of $RW$ and is not
needed. $GA$ (general ability) is latent and cannot be conditioned on; the child
random intercept proxies its time-invariant part, so the slope stays an adjusted
association.

### Interpretation caveats

- *No unmeasured confounder assumption* for $(RW_{\text{post}}, W_{\text{post}})$: strong and not testable — latent general ability is only partially proxied by the child intercept.
- The ERB total is measured with error; non-negligible error attenuates the slope toward zero.
- Dropping unmeasured-exposure rows assumes the missingness is unrelated to the association being estimated (missing-at-random given the adjusters); the fitted row count is reported in `config.json`.
- Across-phase pooling assumes the structural relationship is stable over time; a phase-specific sensitivity fit is available via the `phase_specific_mechanism` flag.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_mechanism.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
